primary.question: What does the term 'Capped Mark' refer to in the University of Essex Undergraduate Rules of Assessment?
primary.expected_answer: A capped mark is a maximum mark that can be awarded for an assessment, and if achieved, no further marks can be gained from re-assessments or supplementary examinations.
primary.actual_answer:
In the context provided, a 'Capped Mark' refers to a situation where module marks or reassessment results are limited to a specific maximum score. For example:

- If you fail up to 60 credits and are offered compulsory reassessment before the start of the next year, your Module Mark(s) will be capped at 40.
- Following reassessment, if you undertake necessary reassessment at the next available opportunity, the Module Aggregate Mark is capped at 40.

This means that regardless of performance in reassessment, the highest mark a student can achieve under these conditions is set to this predetermined limit. Sources: [https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/foundation-year/roa-ug-foundation-year-rules.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/foundation-year/roa-ug-foundation-year-rules.pdf), [https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/specific-courses/roa-ug-incoming-essex-abroad-rules.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/specific-courses/roa-ug-incoming-essex-abroad-rules.pdf)

_Note: this answer is based primarily on "roa-ug-foundation-year-rules.pdf". Your question could also relate to other documents (rules often differ by programme, department, or academic year) - tell me which programme or year you mean if this isn't the right one._

retrieval.retrieval_query: What does the term 'Capped Mark' refer to in the University of Essex Undergraduate Rules of Assessment?

judge.justification: The answer provides specific examples but lacks a general definition of 'Capped Mark' as stated in the reference.

follow_up.question: Can the capped mark be exceeded by a student's performance in any circumstances?
follow_up.expected_answer: No, the capped mark is the highest mark that can be achieved and cannot be exceeded through re-assessment or supplementary examination.
follow_up.actual_answer:
No, the capped mark cannot be exceeded by a student's performance under normal circumstances. According to the provided context:

- If the pre-resubmission module aggregate was below the pass mark, the resubmission can improve the module aggregate up to the pass mark only.
- If the pre-resubmission module aggregate was above the pass mark, the module aggregate is capped at the pre-resubmission aggregate.

This means that regardless of performance in reassessment, a student's final mark cannot exceed the previously calculated cap. Sources: [https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-ot_25.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-ot_25.pdf), [https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/pg_dip_periodontology_25.pdf](https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/pg_dip_periodontology_25.pdf)

_Note: this answer is based primarily on "pg_dip_periodontology_25.pdf". Your question could also relate to other documents (rules often differ by programme, department, or academic year) - tell me which programme or year you mean if this isn't the right one._

primary.question: What does the University of Essex Rules of Assessment cover regarding the final award?
primary.expected_answer: The Rules of Assessment describe the criteria for achieving the final award, which includes whether you have passed all modules and met the requirements to receive a specific classification or exit award.
primary.actual_answer:
The University of Essex Rules of Assessment cover the criteria for achieving the final award. Specifically, they describe what you need to do to progress through your course, pass each year, and the consequences if you don’t pass. These rules are used to calculate results and determine whether you have passed modules and can be awarded credit.

follow_up.question: Can you explain what types of classifications are available under the Rules of Assessment?
follow_up.expected_answer: Under the Rules of Assessment, students can receive different classifications based on their performance, which may include first-class honors, second-class upper division, second-class lower division, third-class honors, and pass or fail.
follow_up.actual_answer:
Under the University of Essex Rules of Assessment, the classification for Honours degrees is detailed in Section A of the rules applicable to your course. Specifically, Section 3.3 of the document "roa-ug-uoe-online-level-one-two-three.pdf" outlines Award Classification for Honours Degrees.

The classifications typically include:
1. First Class Honours
2. Upper Second Class (2:1) Honours
3. Lower Second Class (2:2) Honours
4. Third Class Honours

These classifications are based on the overall grade point average achieved throughout your degree program.

Sources:

primary.question: What is the requirement for passing Year One in the three-year honors degree program according to the rules?
primary.expected_answer: Students must pass all modules taken in year one.
primary.actual_answer:
To pass Year One in a Three-Year Honours Degree programme, you must:

- Pass all core modules.
- Achieve a minimum of 90 credits passed at the first attempt or after reassessment.

primary.expected_answer: Students will have the opportunity to undertake a re-assessment of their work during the subsequent academic year in most cases.

follow_up.question: What happens if a student fails reassessment at the end of Year One?
follow_up.expected_answer: If a student has failed to pass the module at the re-assessment, they will be required to undertake it during the resit assessment period in their subsequent year.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If a student fails reassessment at the end of Year One, they may still be allowed to proceed to the next year under certain conditions:

- They must have passed all core modules.
- They need to have passed a minimum of 90 credits out of the required 120 credits for the year.

Additionally, students can only fail up to 30 non-core credits across their entire degree. If they exceed this limit in Year One, they cannot fail any more credits later on.

primary.question: What are the minimum credits and marks required to progress from Year One to Year Two in an Integrated Masters programme according to this document?
primary.expected_answer: To progress from Year One to Year Two, students must achieve 120 credits at Level 4; pass at least 60 credits at the first attempt; achieve a Year Mark of 50 at the first attempt; and pass core modules. To progress from Year One to Year Two in an Integrated Masters programme, students must:

- Achieve 120 credits at Level 4.

- Achieve a Year Mark of 50 at the first attempt.
- Pass all core modules and meet any additional programme or module-specific requirements.

primary.question: What are the conditions for a student to proceed to the final year if they have passed fewer than 120 credits in Year One?
primary.expected_answer: A student may be allowed to proceed to the Final Year, following an attempt at reassessment, having passed a minimum of 105 credits, provided they have passed all the core modules for the Year.
primary.actual_answer:
If a student has passed fewer than 120 credits in Year One:

- If they failed 30 credits, they must pass 120 credits in Year Two.
- If they failed 15 credits, failure may be condoned in up to 15 non-core credits in Year Two.

Additionally:
- If a total of 15 or fewer credits have been failed across previous years, failure may be condoned in up to 15 non-core credits in the Final Year.
- If no failures occurred in Years One and Two (i.e., passed 240 credits), failure may be condoned in up to 30 non-core credits in the Final Year.

Sources:
[source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/partner-institutions/roa-ug-laksamana-year-1.pdf]
[source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/3-year-honours-degrees/roa-ug-3yr-final-year-rules.pdf]

retrieval.retrieval_query: What are the conditions for a student to proceed to the final year if they have passed fewer than 120 credits in Year One?

judge.justification: The answer provides additional details not asked for and includes conditions beyond the scope of the original question.

follow_up.question: What happens if a student fails more than 15 non-core credits in Year One?
follow_up.expected_answer: A student cannot fail any more credits in their Final Year, and carrying forward failed credits to following years is called condonement.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If a student fails more than 15 non-core credits in Year One:

- They must pass 120 credits in Year Two.

Sources:
[source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/partner-institutions/roa-ug-laksamana-year-1.pdf]

primary.question: What minimum marks are required for passing a taught core module at University of Essex under these rules?
primary.expected_answer: To pass a taught core module, students must achieve a mark of at least 50 and also score at least 40 in both the coursework aggregate mark and exam mark. Marks between 39.5 and 39.9 are rounded up to 40.
primary.actual_answer:
The pass mark for all modules, including core modules, is generally 50 for postgraduate certificates and diplomas according to the provided documents. For undergraduate degrees, the context does not specify an exact numerical pass mark but indicates that core modules must be passed without condonement.

Specifically:
- **Postgraduate Certificate**: The pass mark is 50 (source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-cert-25.pdf)
- **Postgraduate Diploma**: The pass mark is also 50 (source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-dip-25.pdf)
- **Undergraduate Degrees and Graduate Diplomas**: The context does not specify a numerical pass mark but states that core modules must be passed (source_url: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/principle-and-framework/ug-principles-and-framework.pdf)

For undergraduate degrees and graduate diplomas, the exact numerical pass mark is not provided in the given context. However, it's clear that core modules must be passed without condonement.

Sources:
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-cert-25.pdf
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/other-pgt-awards/pg-dip-25.pdf
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/principle-and-framework/ug-principles-and-framework.pdf

primary.question: What are the minimum credit requirements and criteria for passing, gaining a Merit, or achieving Distinction in the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) program at the University of Essex?
primary.expected_answer: To pass, students must obtain 180 credits at Master's level and pass all assessment components in core modules. To gain a Merit, they should have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more. For Distinction, they need to achieve an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.
primary.actual_answer:
For the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) program:

- **Pass**: 
  - Obtain 180 credits at Masters Level (Level 7).
  - Pass all assessment components in core modules.

- **Merit**:
  - In addition to passing, have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more.

- **Distinction**:
  - In addition to passing, have an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.

For exit awards:

- **Postgraduate Diploma (PG Dip)**:
  - Obtain 150 credits at Masters Level.
  - Pass all assessment components in modules except SE760: Research Project.

- **Merit for PG Dip**:
  - Have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more.

- **Distinction for PG Dip**:
  - Have an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.

- **Postgraduate Certificate (PG Cert)**:
  - Obtain 60 credits at Masters Level.
  - Pass all taught core modules.

- **Merit for PG Cert**:
  - Have an overall weighted average mark of 60 or more.

- **Distinction for PG Cert**:
  - Have an overall weighted average mark of 70 or more.

Sources: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-physiotherapy-25.pdf

retrieval.retrieval_query: What are the minimum credit requirements and criteria for passing, gaining a Merit, or achieving Distinction in the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) program at the University of Essex?

judge.justification: The answer is mostly correct but includes unnecessary information about exit awards which were not part of the original question.

follow_up.question: What happens if a student fails to meet the minimum credit requirements for the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) program but still wants to complete their studies?
follow_up.expected_answer: Where a student is unable to complete their studies or does not achieve the required credits to be awarded a Masters, they may be considered for an exit award of either a Postgraduate Diploma or a Postgraduate Certificate.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If a student is unable to complete the full 180 credits required for the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) or does not achieve the necessary credits, they may be considered for an exit award such as a Postgraduate Diploma (PG Dip) or Postgraduate Certificate (PG Cert). 

- **Postgraduate Diploma in Physiotherapy (Preregistration)**:
  - Obtain 150 credits at Masters Level.
  - Pass all assessment components except SE760: Research Project.

- **Postgraduate Certificate**:
  - Obtain 60 credits at Masters Level.
  - Pass all taught core modules.

Departments will specify which taught modules must be passed to obtain these exit awards. The Postgraduate Diploma in Physiotherapy (Preregistration) leads to eligibility for registration with the Health and Care Professions Council, while other exit awards may not provide this eligibility.

Sources: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-physiotherapy-25.pdf

retrieval.retrieval_query: What happens if a student fails to meet the minimum credit requirements for the MSc Physiotherapy (Pre-registration) program but still wants to complete their studies?

judge.justification: The answer is mostly correct but includes unnecessary details not present in the reference answer.

primary.question: What is the maximum number of credits that can be carried forward for progression in the MSc Periodontology or MSc Advanced Periodontal Practice programs?
primary.expected_answer: Students are permitted to progress to Year 2 or Year 3 trailing a maximum of 30 credits.
primary.actual_answer:
The maximum number of credits that can be carried forward for progression in the MSc Periodontology or MSc Advanced Periodontal Practice programs is 30 credits. follow_up.expected_answer: An in-year Board of Examiners meeting may be convened, and the Board will either withdraw the student or offer additional assessment opportunities.

If a student fails an assignment on the second submission, an in-year Board of Examiners meeting is convened. primary.question: What is the pass mark for all modules of the MSc Human Resource Management program according to these rules?
primary.expected_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the Masters degree is 50.
primary.actual_answer: The pass mark for all modules of the MSc Human Resource Management program is 50. Sources: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-human-resource-management-25.pdf

follow_up.question: What happens if a student fails a core module and how does it affect their progression to Year Two?
follow_up.expected_answer: Students will still be permitted to progress to Year Two if they have failed a core module, but only if they can still be offered further reassessment attempts or are eligible for an exit award of a Postgraduate Diploma or a Postgraduate Certificate.
follow_up.actual_answer:
If a student fails a core module, they are required to undertake reassessment at the first available opportunity. This reassessment will normally be undertaken during the summer vacation of Year One. If the reassessment is also failed, students will not be given another attempt unless there are extenuating circumstances accepted by the Board of Examiners which permit another assessment attempt.

If a student still fails after this reassessment and does not have extenuating circumstances accepted by the Board of Examiners, they may no longer be on track for a Masters degree. However, they will only be permitted to proceed if they are still eligible for an exit award based on their credits achieved up to that point.

Sources: https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/pgt/2025-26/department-specific/msc-human-resource-management-25.pdf

retrieval.retrieval_query: What happens if a student fails a core module in the MSc Human Resource Management program and how does it affect their progression to Year Two?

judge.justification: The answer is mostly correct but omits the specific condition that students can still progress to Year Two if they are eligible for an exit award.

primary.expected_answer: Learning outcomes identify what a successful student is expected to know and understand, together with the skills that they should be able to demonstrate on successful completion of the award. primary.question: What are the general principles for reassessment according to the rules of assessment document?
primary.expected_answer: The general principles for reassessment apply throughout all years, including Year One, Two, Three, and the Final Year, but specific details vary by year.
primary.actual_answer:
According to the rules of assessment documents:

- You must take reassessment if you fail any modules after completing all assessments but have achieved a Year Mark or Term marks of 20 or above.
- The Board of Examiners can permit up to two further attempts at any module, with Module Marks capped at 40 for these attempts unless there are substantiated Extenuating Circumstances (for Chula).
- You must complete reassessment before progressing to the next Year or Stage of study, except in cases where trailed reassessment is offered.
- The Board will offer all available reassessment opportunities and use your previous higher mark if you attempt a module again.

Sources:
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/partner-institutions/chula---original-course-structure.pdf
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/partner-institutions/iep-portobello-year-2.pdf
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/3-year-honours-degrees/roa-ug-3yr-year-2-rules.pdf
- https://www.essex.ac.uk/-/media/documents/directories/academic-section/rules-of-assessment/ug/current/partner-institutions/iep-portobello-year-1.pdf

_Note: this answer is based primarily on "chula---original-course-structure.pdf". Your question could also relate to other documents (rules often differ by programme, department, or academic year) - tell me which programme or year you mean if this isn't the right one._
